management_notes:
CEO and co-founder Rex Motton brings approximately 40 years of global exploration experience as a registered Australian geologist, Chartered Professional of the AusIMM, and Fellow of the Society of Economic Geology. His technical credentials are appropriate for a Bendigo Zone gold explorer and he holds a meaningful founder position as a major shareholder. CFO Brenda Nowak brings 30+ years of securities and corporate finance experience.

The company commenced trading on the TSXV on March 4, 2026 — meaning it has been a public entity for approximately 6 weeks as of this scorecard date. With no drill results, no resource estimate, and no operational track record as a public company, the management team cannot be assessed on execution quality. The Fiore Group (Frank Giustra's Vancouver firm) backing is notable — Fiore has backed NexGold, Aris Gold, West Red Lake Gold, and Nations Royalty among others — providing capital markets expertise, but the list of Fiore-backed companies includes both successes and promotionally structured vehicles that delivered mixed results.

Advisor James Hutton, appointed April 9, 2026, is described as a co-founder with major shareholder status. The dual-founder structure and very recent board governance initiatives suggest the company is still establishing its governance framework. No VP Exploration was identified in available sources.
geology_notes:
The Steiglitz Gold Project (~80 km west of Melbourne, Victoria, Australia) is in its earliest exploration stage: the maiden 2026 field program commenced March 2026 with systematic soil sampling (247 samples from the Hanover Fault Zone) and geological mapping. A work plan for Phase 1 scout drilling has been submitted for regulatory approval, with drilling targeted for Q2 2026. No drill holes have been completed. No assay results have been received. No resource estimate (NI 43-101 or JORC) exists.

The historical context is promising: the Steiglitz district produced approximately 250,800 oz Au at a recovered grade of 38 g/t Au during shallow mining between 1855 and 1911 — a strong geological signal indicating high-grade mineralisation in the area. The deposit style is orogenic gold (Bendigo Zone) — saddle reef and anticlinal structures in a proven gold belt. The nearby comparables are world-class: Fosterville Mine (Agnico Eagle, 600,000+ oz/yr at exceptional grades), and Southern Cross Gold's Sunday Creek discovery (one of the best high-grade epizonal gold discoveries globally in recent years).

The secondary Pheasant Creek project (~75 km northeast of Melbourne, exploration licence application, same geology as Sunday Creek) adds potential upside, but the application stage means no ground access for work programs. At this stage — soil sampling only, pre-first-hole — the geological score reflects the complete absence of drill-defined mineralisation, regardless of the district pedigree.
capital_notes:
Crossroads Gold raised C$5.24M in its go-public brokered private placement (26.2 million subscription receipts at C$0.20/share) and commenced trading with approximately 63.5 million shares outstanding at a market cap of approximately C$13-15M. Estimated working capital after IPO costs is approximately C$3-4M — adequate for a Phase 1 soil sampling and maiden scout drilling program, but with limited runway beyond two field seasons without additional financing.

The share structure is reasonably tight at 63.5M shares for a grassroots TSXV junior. Full warrant and option schedules were not publicly detailed in available sources at time of this research. The go-public financing structure (subscription receipts converting to shares on TSXV listing) is standard for TSXV IPOs. No debt.

The primary capital concern is the limited runway: with approximately C$3-4M in working capital, the company has approximately 12-18 months of funding before requiring additional capital at the current exploration expenditure rate. A Phase 1 scout drilling program of 2,000-3,000 metres in Victoria would consume approximately C$1-1.5M in direct drilling costs, leaving minimal buffer for unexpected delays or negative results that require a larger program.
catalyst_notes:
The only near-term catalyst is the commencement and results of Phase 1 scout drilling at Steiglitz. The work plan was submitted for regulatory approval in early April 2026 with drilling targeted for Q2 2026, but drilling commencement is contingent on Victoria Department of Mines and Petroleum (DMP) approving the work plan — a gating factor outside the company's control with an uncertain timeline.

Assuming regulatory approval in Q2 2026, Phase 1 assay results would be expected Q3 2026 at earliest (6-8 week laboratory turnaround). These initial results will determine whether the soil sampling anomalies correlate with bedrock mineralisation — a go/no-go decision point for Phase 2 drilling.

Beyond Phase 1 drilling, no additional material catalysts are expected in the 12-month horizon: no resource estimate is possible without multiple drill programs, and the Pheasant Creek project (the highest-potential comparable to Sunday Creek geology) is at the exploration licence application stage.
acquisition_notes:
With an enterprise value of approximately C$9-12M and no drill results, Crossroads Gold has no per-ounce acquisition value to assess. The investment is purely an optionality call option on the Bendigo Zone address and the possibility that Phase 1 drilling identifies high-grade gold mineralisation comparable to the historical mining grades (38 g/t Au recovered) or the nearby Southern Cross Gold Sunday Creek discovery.

The Southern Cross Gold Sunday Creek discovery (same Bendigo Zone, same Victoria state, similar geological model) provides the relevant comparable upside scenario: SXGC's market cap reached C$300-500M following initial high-grade discovery results — a 20-50x multiple from where CRG currently trades. However, Southern Cross had a significantly more experienced team with pre-existing geological data on the Sunday Creek site, and the discovery process took multiple field seasons after extensive historical data compilation.

No M&A interest has been identified. Fiore Group backing provides capital markets access but no strategic mining major involvement. The Bendigo Zone is a globally recognised gold belt (Fosterville, Agnico Eagle's flagship mine) and a successful discovery would attract immediate major-producer attention, but this outcome requires successful exploration execution across multiple phases.
analyst_summary:
AVOID (10/25). Crossroads Gold is in its earliest possible stage — a March 2026 IPO with no drill results, no resource estimate, and soil sampling barely commenced at the Steiglitz Gold Project. The Bendigo Zone geological address is legitimate (Fosterville, Sunday Creek nearby), and Rex Motton's technical credentials are appropriate. However, the Verdict Framework requires evidence across all five factors, and three of five (geology, catalyst proximity, acquisition value) reflect the complete absence of exploration data. The C$13-15M market cap is speculative even at this small scale.

The primary risks are the absence of any mineralisation data from drilling, the limited C$3-4M treasury that constrains the number of drill holes possible before a financing is required, and the regulatory gating for drill permit approval in Victoria. The Fiore Group connection adds capital markets execution risk: Fiore-backed companies have a mixed track record, and the promotional structure of TSXV IPOs in this price range can attract retail attention disproportionate to geological merit.

The key catalyst to watch is the first Phase 1 drill assay results from Steiglitz, expected Q3 2026. If results show high-grade (10+ g/t Au) intersections in bedrock consistent with the historical 38 g/t recovered grade, this stock could re-rate dramatically and move to WATCH or BUY territory. An AVOID verdict at this stage is appropriate — revisit after first drill results when geological merit can be assessed.
